programme.title: Xploration Animal Science

programme.title: Mitchell Pehlke Lacrosse Show
programme.desc: Welcome to Episode 9 (Season 3) of the Mitchell Pehlke Lacrosse Show presented by Duke Cannon. In this episode, we welcome back Syracuse attackman Joey Spallina after breaking the program record for career points. The crew also caught up with PLL Draft Analyst Adam Lamberti to get his reaction to the 2026 class and early ranking of the 2027 class. Kev and Hoagie debated the Big Ten naming four regular-season co-champions, while Mitch tried to get to the bottom of where Doogs was this week.

programme.desc: A seemingly healthy marriage is threatened by deadly secrets as the couple discovers that smiles can be deceiving, and kisses can be poison.

programme.desc: An African American teen has an explosive relationship with his tyrannical religious fanatic stepfather in 1930s Harlem.

programme.title: Stock Option
programme.desc: Sparks fly between a homeless stockbroker and a famous model after the man saves her from a robbery attempt.

programme.title: Life of a King

programme.title: Clover
programme.desc: When her father dies shortly after his remarriage, a young African American girl struggles to cope while left in the care of her new, white stepmom.

programme.title: Don't Shoot
programme.desc: Following the shooting of an unarmed Black teen by two White officers, a city is divided in the fight to seek social justice and reform.

programme.desc: The true story behind the legendary Motown musical sensation is brought to life in this touching miniseries.

programme.title: The Love We Had
programme.desc: After one decision destroys the life of a young couple, it continues to ruin their other relationships and everything they hold sacred.

programme.title: One Love
programme.desc: A rasta musician meets and falls for a gospel singer when they both enter a music contest in Kingston, Jamaica. However, her father has other ideas.

programme.title: Uncle P
programme.desc: Multimillionaire hip-hop superstar Master P is thrown headfirst into fatherhood in the suburbs when he becomes the guardian of his sister’s children.

programme.title: The Fantastic World of D.C. Collins
programme.desc: A U.N. diplomat's (Bernie Casey) pint-size son (Gary Coleman) plays hero in his daydreams and with spies who are real.

programme.title: The Temptations
programme.desc: The true story behind the legendary Motown musical sensation is brought to life in this touching miniseries.

programme.desc: As their conflicting ideas begin to take a toll on their relationship, an investigator plans to prove his lover, an assistant DA, wrong about a case.

programme.desc: This year, the delightfully dysfunctional Cleaver family will settle their differences once and for all... with a few misadventures along the way.

programme.desc: The sibling rivalry between a respected businesswoman's two daughters threatens everything she has built in her city.

programme.title: EscapeCation

programme.title: Angel and Demon

programme.title: Deadly Squatters

programme.title: The Agony and the Ecstasy
programme.desc: When rival gangster Derrick Smith is released from prison, the beef with Armand Rossi explodes into an all-out war in Fountain City, MO. After a hit on his family, Armand goes into killer mode. Derrick and Vic Moore team up for revenge, but Rossi.

programme.title: Cougar
programme.desc: In suburban Atlanta, Donna's son Brandon bonds with a mysterious new neighbor. As their connection turns deadly, Donna races to save him.

programme.title: In Transit
programme.desc: Two friends living a life of drugs and women in New York City learn that fast money has its benefits, but may be more trouble than it's worth.

programme.title: It Is What It Is
programme.desc: Victoria, a sophisticated young woman, is trying to find happiness with a good man in the midst of drugs and violence in the mean streets of KC.

programme.title: Hidden Agenda
programme.desc: When a popular football star dies after an after party, a woman goes on the hunt to find his killer.

programme.title: Fractured
programme.desc: A thrilling narrative that follows a woman’s struggle to keep her life together after discovering that her husband has been unfaithful.

programme.title: Busted and Disgusted
programme.desc: Promise is envious of Faith and her success, so she seeks to claim some of that happiness by any means necessary, no matter the cost.

programme.title: Bissonnet
programme.desc: Newly broke, Drea turns to the streets to hustle. But fast money and life on Houston's notorious Bissonnet Street come at a high price.

programme.title: 10 Kilos in the Trunk
programme.desc: When her friend gets locked up, Monica agrees to deliver a very important package in the trunk of her car, but a series of obstacles gets in the way.

programme.title: South Side Hero
programme.desc: After serving a 10-year sentence on a RICO charge, reformed gangster Tommy "Ace" Crown returns home to find his 15-year old daughter Sierra missing after she ran away with a man she met online. When the police decline to help, Ace is determined to track her down himself.

programme.title: Double Standards

programme.title: Jeff Garcia: Living the Dream
programme.desc: The Latino comedian and voiceover artist brings his signature style to jokes about family, cars, white people, marriage, and his run-ins with the law.

programme.title: Kim McVicar: Please Notice Me
programme.desc: The whip-smart former backup dancer discusses the merits of crawling for rappers, flirting at her father’s funeral, and so much more.

programme.title: Gregg Rogell: Balloon Animals
programme.desc: Gregg Rogell, the master of the slow burn, twists and stretches modern life in a comedic journey.

programme.title: Jay Mohr: American Treasure
programme.desc: The popular comedian tells deeply personal stories from his fascinating life and presents his famously funny celebrity impressions and observations.

programme.title: Andy Woodhull: World's Greatest Stepdad
programme.desc: With five albums and appearances on “Conan'' “Late Late Night,” and Comedy Central, he was the first comic to debut on Jimmy Fallon’s “Tonight Show.”

programme.title: Bryan Callen: Man Class
programme.desc: In his first one-hour comedy special, the former MADtv comic gives his unique take on how to be a real man in our ever-changing modern society.

programme.title: Bryan Callen: Never Grow Up
programme.desc: The comedian examines the way our modern culture works, and wonders why we feel the need to view each other in such a narrow box.

programme.title: Godfrey: Regular Black
programme.desc: The hilarious, fearless comic discusses college, public transportation, and more in this comedy special performed in his hometown of Chicago.

programme.title: Zarna Garg: One in a Billion
programme.desc: Zarna, an Indian-Immigrant mom, has plenty to joke about.

programme.title: Sinbad: Make Me Wanna Holla
programme.desc: A stand-up special covering the everything from the life advice his parents gave him to people with no talent having clothing lines and more.

programme.title: Sinbad: Where U Been
programme.desc: Sinbad hits the stage hard as he speaks truth to the burning subjects of the day, from poverty and politics to where he's been keeping himself.

programme.title: Gabriel Iglesias: I'm Not Fat... I'm Fluffy
programme.desc: The comic known for wearing his signature Hawaiian shirts covers everything from his fondness for Paul Rodriguez to his own "fluffiness."

programme.title: Gabriel Iglesias: Hot and Fluffy
programme.desc: Filmed at the Fox Theater in Bakersfield, California the comedian and well-known favorite to television viewers as being on "Last Comic Standing," performs impressions and silly voices.

programme.title: Gabriel Iglesias: Aloha Fluffy
programme.desc: The comic discusses his crazy world of family, friends, and world travels.

programme.title: Jim Gaffigan: The Pale Tourist - Canadian American
programme.desc: Jim Gaffigan performs in Canada and has some very strong opinions on poutine, Saskatchewan, health care and the pronunciation of the city of Regina.

programme.title: Jim Gaffigan: Noble Ape
programme.desc: The stand-up comedy star describes how his wife faced a medical crisis, and he attempted to hold down the fort for their five children.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: Spark of Insanity
programme.desc: Jeff and his felt friends Walter, Melvin the Superhero Guy, Peanut, and others share their latest and greatest thoughts about our times.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: Unhinged in Hollywood
programme.desc: Previously unseen stand-up comedy by Jeff Dunham; sketches that include popular characters; Brad Paisley performs; special appearances by comic Chris Parnell and former UFC champion Chuck Liddell.

programme.title: Jeff Garcia: Low Budget Madness
programme.desc: Shot live at San Manuel Indian Bingo and Casino in Highland, CA, Jeff Garcia's Low Budget Madness is packed with hilarious sketches that will leave you in stitches and remind you why audiences all over the world can't get a enough of Jeff Garcia!

programme.title: James Gregory: Food Is Not Fatal
programme.desc: On a mission to spread the good news of “real butter,” comedian James Gregory riffs on chicken legs, hotter summers, and more in this special.

programme.title: Jeff Allen: Honor Thy Wife
programme.desc: The laugh-out-loud funny stand up comedian focuses his special on the hilarious highs and relatable lows of everyday married life.

programme.title: Iliza Shlesinger: Freezing Hot
programme.desc: The acclaimed Last Comic Standing winner explains how women hate being cold, but love cold weather, and other hilarious observations.

programme.title: Daniel Webb: Hoe's Parade Live at the Rose Bowl
programme.desc: The comic discusses his frustrations with ​​Karen Pence, his loving relationship with his dog, and how jazzed he is to perform at the historic venue.

programme.title: Gary Owen: Black Famous
programme.desc: This last year has been unlike any other: COVID, lockdowns, and for Gary, it has also included a divorce and family insanity unlike any other; up until now, Gary hasn't been able to speak about what really happened.

programme.title: Gary Owen: Breakin' Out the Park
programme.desc: In this brutally honest stand-up performance, comedian Gary Owen confesses his hilarious history of growing up in and around trailer parks.

programme.title: Gary Owen: #DoinWhatIDo
programme.desc: The so-called “Funniest Serviceman in America” draws from his multiracial experience to lambast culture wars on social media and in his own home.

programme.title: Jay Mohr: Altamont
programme.desc: Comedian Jay Mohr tackles subjects from unexpected visitors, bringing up children to working with Chris Farley on SNL in his new stand-up special.

programme.title: Jay Mohr: American Treasure
programme.desc: The popular comedian tells deeply personal stories from his fascinating life and presents his famously funny celebrity impressions and observations.

programme.title: Kevin Hart Presents: Lil Rel Howery - RELevent
programme.desc: Hart hosts the high-energy comic who riffs on his upbringing, raising kids, and public transportation, proving he can be hilarious and relevant.

programme.title: Kylie Brakeman: Linda Hollywood's Big Night
programme.desc: Character comedian Kylie Brakeman teaches an acting masterclass as Linda Hollywood, a problematic, old school talent agent on a mission to spread her industry wisdom-and save her own career in the process.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: Minding the Monsters
programme.desc: The comedian and ventriloquist extraordinaire, along with this felt friends, visits a haunted house where hilarious chaos ensues.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: All Over the Map
programme.desc: A comedy set and travelogue by comedian Jeff Dunham as he and his ventriloquist’s dummies perform globally from Iceland and London to Israel.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: Arguing With Myself
programme.desc: Join Jeff and his outspoken felt pals: Walter, Peanut, José Jalapeño, Bubba J, and Sweet Daddy Dee for an evening of laughs and observations.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: I'm With Cupid
programme.desc: Ventriloquist and stand-up comedian Jeff Dunham performs an all-Valentine's Day-themed special.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: Controlled Chaos
programme.desc: The ventriloquist comedian provides fresh, hilarious material with his favorite puppets; Walter, Peanut, and two brand new characters.

programme.title: Tom Segura: Completely Normal
programme.desc: Radio, television and live performance veteran Tom Segura shares a few off-hand appraisals on hotels and hobbies with a cool and thoughtful demeanor.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Actor/screenwriter Peter Fonda opens up about being compared to his father and sister.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Catch outrageous flirting as Angie Dickinson denies rumors about her and singer Julio Iglesias.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Ed Begley Jr. is joined by Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom's Kate Capshaw and comedian Bobby Kelton.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Oscar winner and activist Susan Sarandon recalls her first communion.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Christie Brinkley chats about hard work posing for SI's swimsuit issue and upcoming marriage to Billy Joel.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Tom Snyder confesses it’s hard being Johnny’s guest and describes his life in California.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Leonard Waxdeck returns to the panel, with more incredible bird callers in tow.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Father Guido Sarducci stops by the set to talk about his pet dog and starting his own church.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Actor/pilot John Travolta on flying lessons, dropping out of high school and getting his first part.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Superman himself Christopher Reeve describes taking acting classes with Robin Williams.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Betty White wanted to be a forest ranger in the 1940s but that gig was off-limits to women in the early 1940s.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Johnny's monologue about baseball opening day is followed by Robin Williams crawling and imitating his infant son. Phyllis Newman shares that her mother was psychic.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Richard Pryor pokes fun at his failure at marriage and reflects on his new sobriety.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Angie Dickinson chats about New Year’s Eve parties, New Year’s resolutions, her first date and more.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Dudley Moore got his star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame then sings a song. Bob Uecker's nickname was "Stinko."

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Tim Conway and Harry Anderson stop by to chat with Johnny.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Steven Wright talks about everything from his girlfriend to filming a small part in his first movie.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Jim Fowler, zoologist and host of the famed "Wild Kingdom" program, brings a vulture and two opossums.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: The ultimate Hollywood bad guy, Jack Palance chats with Johnny about his break-out role in Shane (1949).

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Carl chats about wife Estelle, a nose injury, forgetfulness, and When Harry Met Sally, by his son Rob Reiner.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Teri shows clip from Oscar-nom’d Tootsie performance, parses success in show biz vs. recognition as actress.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Tony Randall chats role as Philadelphia judge on The Tony Randall Show. Also tells Johnny he’s quit the booze.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Jim Fowler brings out an alligator and crocodile. Plus, Rodney Dangerfield has Johnny in stitches.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Tony Randall spills on early experiences with girls, first time he bought condoms. Opines on sex ed. classes.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Carlin and Carson talk about writing a sequel to the Bible. Plus Susan Sullivan and Sheryl Lee Ralph appear.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show

programme.desc: As Darlene grapples with a life-altering offer, a conversation with David turns her world more upside-down.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal USA
programme.desc: The contestant picks one case which is theirs to keep, if they choose, until its unsealing at the end of the game. (29 out of 39)

programme.desc: From singing Pastors to Wookies, this week is jammed pack with Characters, who battle it out on the Qualifier with amusing attempts at the inconceivable Field Goal Swing. Its no easy task to earn a spot in the Wipeout Zone this week with the Dreadmill, sorting the men from the boys. Contestants must dodge the wrecking ball while shooting basketballs. Although there is one man who came prepared, Dodgeball Champion Dan Bennett, whose “Duck, Dive, Dip, Dodge and Dunk” training comes into play.

programme.desc: Fashion comes to Wipeout Australia as we meet Adam “God’s Gift” Marshall, a self styled Ad man who conquers the Qualifier. Other Catwalk moments include the Grandpa in his Red Speedos and a Rainbow Bright Personal Ad. If jumping a revolving arm while balancing on a pedestal above water isn’t enough for you then you’ll love this weeks Sweeper. Contestants are armed with Dodgeballs, to be deployed at each other, creating horrific hits and spectacular spills. The Dizzy Dummy claims its first victim as Dummy Tummy rejects a contestant and the Wipeout Zone becomes a battle of young and old.

We visit RHS Wisley to learn why autumn leaves are such a precious commodity for our gardens, and in the final instalment of her botany series, Carol Klein celebrates the glorious displays put on by flowering plants to attract pollinators.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team are in Aylesbury with Michelle and Alistair. They have a small back garden but they need it to work hard for them providing an entertainment and relaxation space, and somewhere to burn off steam for their two daughters, who both have autism.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team are in Norwich at the garden of Taj and Rachel, who have been together for nearly four years and have a puppy, and a baby on the way. They would love to challenge the designers to tackle the slope at the bottom of their garden.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Elaine in Lymington loves gardening, but her front garden is out of control, with loads of messy shrubs, an out-of-control conifer and a front door that the postman can't find. Can the team give purpose to the chaotic front space that matches Elaine's own dramatic style?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the autumn light bathes Longmeadow, Monty is harvesting pumpkins in time for Halloween, adding a berberis to his orchard border and planting out strawberries. Frances Tophill shows how her garden in Devon has been developing.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: It is an exciting week at Longmeadow as Monty makes a start on his new woodland garden. Advolly Richmond looks at the history of Gertrude Jekyll, and Carol Klein revels in the displays at RHS Rosemoor.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Frances Tophill is back at Damson Farm, lending a hand with autumn jobs in the garden. She learns of a recipe for making an architectural compost cake, plants a container with evergreens and spring bulbs, and takes hard wood cuttings from a tree.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty Don visits two sets of ambitious gardeners, each facing challenges on a very different scale. One is a tiny urban courtyard in Hackney and the other is a outdoor area for a community.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty meets two sets of gardeners who have unimaginable ambitions for their outdoor spaces. In Essex Denise has never ventured beyond the decking area behind her house. Meanwhile in Ipswich, a father and daughter plan to build a terraced allotment.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty comes to the rescue of two couples in need of help transforming their rubble filled back gardens into havens of peace and tranquillity. They are in serious need of help and advice but can Monty set them on the right track?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As usual, Monty is at Longmeadow where he will set out his plans for the coming year. Monty reviews the effects the winter weather has had on his garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the gardening year gets underway at Longmeadow, Monty is tidying up his ornamental grass border, pruning roses and planning for summer scent. Joe Swift continues his series on trees when he gives tips for how to plant a tree for future success.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As part of his planning for summer Monty begins to sow annual climbers and gets started on tidying and mulching his borders. In the last of Joe Swift's series on trees, he learns how and when to prune them, and why. We also meet clematis enthusiast, Mike Brown, whose collection fills his garden with colour all summer long, and amongst the brutalist architecture of London's Barbican, Nick Bailey, head gardener of the Chelsea Physic Garden, discovers a hidden tropical oasis.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The first big gardening weekend of the year gets underway with Monty dividing perennials and giving plenty of tips for how we can kick-start the garden for the season ahead. As millions of bedding plants are poised for planting in our pots and borders, Joe Swift visits a major grower to find out how they are produced and what drives the demand.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty gets to work in his wildlife garden to ensure that there are plenty of plants in flower for early pollinators. Carol Klein meets Stuart Donachie who has embraced every planting opportunity in his shady garden in Herefordshire, and Frances Tophill invites viewers to grow easy summer vegetables along with her as she trials varieties for taste and productivity at RHS Rosemoor.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty starts a new project, a cutting garden, and gives advice on what to grow to provide plenty of cut flowers for gorgeous summer bouquets. We visit Cumbria to meet Jack Gott, who has been passionate about that most flamboyant of flowers, the dahlia, for more than forty years and has over a thousand plants in his garden; and James Alexander-Sinclair celebrates a harbinger of Spring, the ephemeral cherry blossom, at Alnwick Castle in Northumberland.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty gets his new vegetable garden underway by making raised beds. He also offers advice on how to cut back herbs. Nick Bailey marks the 400th anniversary of the death of William Shakespeare when he investigates the fact and the fiction behind the Bard's potions and poisons, at the Chelsea Physic Garden; and we visit Pashley Manor in East Sussex to unearth the story behind the planting of over 25,000 tulips.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the growing season picks up a pace, Monty is sowing vegetables and planting out the scented plants, which he brought back from the Malvern Spring Festival. Carol is delving into the hedgerows and rooting at the bases of walls, fences and trees as she begins her journey to investigate why plants thrive in challenging conditions. We also return to Sissinghurst to find out about the changes Troy Scott-Smith is making to the world famous white garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow Monty's focus is on summer when he plants up containers for colour in the Jewel Garden. He also turns his attention to overcrowded ornamental grasses which, now they are beginning to put on growth, need to be split and replanted. Continuing her search for finding the right plant for the right place, Carol takes a close look at why plants survive in the cracks and crevices of walls and gives her recommendations for garden plants that will thrive in similar conditions.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: This week we are celebrating the work of the army of volunteers, which keeps gardens up and down the country looking their best for visitors. Frances Tophill continues her vegetable trials at RHS Rosemoor in Devon, when she plants out her allotment with the help of RHS volunteers and we visit the Bodnant Garden in North Wales to find out how the volunteers there guide visitors through the world famous Laburnum Arch.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Tender vegetables, bedding plants and bees are the focus of Monty's gardening at Longmeadow as he plants out squashes and scented annuals, and harvests honey. Adam Frost is in London, looking at how small spaces in the metropolis can be utilised to make gardens for wildlife, food and relaxation. Also, we visit North Wales to meet a man with a passion for prehistoric plants.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the longest day of the year approaches, there's more time to be outside and enjoy the garden and Monty is no exception. Although some vegetables do not respond well to a late sowing, there are others that do and Monty gives his tips on late croppers to sow now. Carol Klein and Joe Swift make a visit to Gardeners' World Live in Birmingham to revel in the hundreds of summer flowering plants on display and to find design tips for small gardens.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Whilst celebrating the glory of summer flowers at Longmeadow, Monty has plenty of tips, from pruning and propagation techniques for different types of clematis to troubleshooting problems with roses. Carol Klein wades through streams and marshes to show us how gardeners can emulate nature when choosing the right water plants for our gardens, and we visit Norfolk to revel in an extraordinary collection of iris.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is reaping the rewards of the summer when he begins to harvest crops from the vegetable garden and gives tips on extending the flower season in the borders. Carol takes a trip to the seaside to discover why some plants thrive, despite being assaulted by salt-laden winds. We make a final visit to Sissinghurst, to catch up with Troy Scott-Smith and the changes that have been made to the garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty demonstrates that it is not too late to start growing vegetables by showing us which types to sow now, and adds some summer colour to the Spring Garden by sinking plants in pots into the borders. We meet a hosta National Collection holder, who shares the secrets of his propagating success, and Rachel de Thame pays a visit to the garden of theatre impresario Sir Cameron Mackintosh.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Earlier in the year Monty put aside a patch of his garden to grow his own cut flowers and this week he returns to assess the results and harvest his first crop of colourful blooms. We pay a visit to an organic flower farm to find out how, from seed to harvest, the process of growing plays a large part in improving well-being.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As summer gets into full swing, there is plenty for Monty to be doing at Longmeadow. He gets to grips with summer pruning as he tackles his espaliered pear trees. Nature's bounty is very much in evidence at Lord Rothschild's garden at Waddesdon Manor, where we get an exclusive look behind the scenes. Also, Joe Swift ponders ways of putting a contemporary twist on traditional bedding plants.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Now that the rambling roses have finished flowering, Monty turns his attention to pruning and tying in new growth for a good display next year. On the 20th anniversary of the death of Geoff Hamilton, Adam Frost returns to the famous `Gardeners' World' location, Barnsdale, to pay tribute and to revisit some his own early gardening memories. Also, we travel to Abergavenny to visit the garden of a sweet pea enthusiast to get her top tips for growing the best blooms.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Joe Swift pays a visit to the Northumberland garden of Chris Mullin who, after over twenty years as a Member of Parliament, has turned his attention to the renovation of his walled garden. And National Trust head gardener, Alan Power, catches up with the transformation of Shakespeare's New Place garden in Stratford-upon-Avon.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Now that birds are no longer nesting in hedges, now is the time to get them looking trim and neat and Monty has plenty of tips on how to cut them so that they grow well. Alan Power visits Croome Park in Worcestershire to discover more about one of the first great landscapes of Capability Brown and we visit a couple in Gloucestershire whose ornamental grass garden was inspired by the Far East.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the bank holiday ahead there's plenty of opportunity to catch up with some essential jobs in the garden and Monty gives a masterclass on how to look after and prune soft fruit to ensure maximum production next year. Frances Tophill is at RHS Rosemoor where she is catching up on her vegetable trials which she started earlier in the year, and Alan Power visits Hestercombe Gardens in Somerset to find out more about the discovery of a long lost gladiolus.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty and the regular team are joined by some new faces offering tips, advice and inspiration from gardens large and small across the country. In this programme Monty is at Longmeadow catching up with some seasonal jobs, and also getting out and about for an inspiring visit to the longest herbaceous border in the world.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is at Longmeadow giving advice on how to deal with unruly roses, taking cuttings of herbs and planting a new tree. Carol Klein continues her series on plant families, exploring some of the most popular relations of the rose and we pay a visit to an orchard in Worcestershire to find out how enthusiasts are caring for one of the rose family members - the plum.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Roxy and Steve live in Shrewsbury with their young children, but their garden has nothing exciting for children. It's so bad that when their children's nine year-old cousin came round to play, she complained there was nothing to do in the garden.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Sheena and James have saved hard to create their dream garden. The only trouble is, despite being a gardening enthusiast, Sheena has run aground in her efforts to create a garden that is green and tree-filled but also a nod to their coastal area.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Katie and Christian and their kids Max and Olivia live in Bitterne, near Southampton. They are suffering from a classic case of 'building work ruined the garden'. In fact, mum Katie calls it a `catastrophe' of a garden. Can Charlie and the Rich brothers save the day, despite having a budget of only £2,500? The garden also has to work as a space for both day and night. Christian works shifts, so he would like to use it late into the evening.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty is full steam ahead creating a new woodland garden from scratch. Adam Frost celebrates the drama, versatility and reliability of ornamental grasses.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With winter fast approaching, Monty has dahlias to lift and store, leaf mould to make, and tulips and wallflowers to plant in pots. He also takes root cuttings from a favourite herbaceous perennial.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: After a busy winter, Monty plants up pots for early colour, shows how to move a clematis and sows veg for the year ahead. Adam Frost visits Ventnor Botanic Garden on the Isle of Wight. A gardener turned a concrete slab into her dream with containers.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Two gardeners, each living on their own, are hoping Monty can help them. In Blackpool, Alex dreams of turning her boring back yard into a magical enchanted forest. Also in Bristol, Ross has taken on a very over-grown allotment.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: In Leyton, Tariq and Sairah want to turn their dumping ground of a back yard into a sumptuous award-winning garden, suitable for entertaining friends and family. Both have competitive jobs in the banking sector and are used to winning at work but when it comes to gardening, they don't know where to begin.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: One couple battle to turn a clay-clogged building site in Sussex into a piece of Portugal. Another tackle a forest of overgrowth in their new back garden in Cadeby. They are determined to create a productive space, something they have always wanted.

programme.title: Monty Don's Adriatic Gardens
programme.desc: Monty travels to Europe and explores the influence of the Venetians on gardening. To begin with, he explores Venice on one of the city's famous barges, stepping out to visit green spaces large and small that are often hidden from view behind high walls.

programme.title: Monty Don's Adriatic Gardens
programme.desc: Monty begins in the city of Trieste, close to Italy's border with Croatia, to visit a public garden first created by an obsessive royal plantsman more than 150 years ago, which is now being carefully restored. He then heads down the Adriatic coast through Croatia to pick up the trail that the Venetians followed, ending his journey on the beautiful island of Lopud, near Dubrovnik.

programme.title: Monty Don's Adriatic Gardens
programme.desc: Monty begins the last leg of his journey in Corfu, a Greek island with strong Venetian links, including olive trees planted by them that are still grown today. Here, Monty visits a spectacular garden made by an Englishman with Greek connections.

programme.title: Monty Don's British Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don travels from the north west coast of Scotland to the north east coast of England, from historic reconstructions to modern re-inventions. He visits some of the most inhospitable places in the country and finds gardens that are flourishing.

programme.title: Monty Don's British Gardens
programme.desc: Monty travels across some of the country's landscapes, heading across the north of England to Northern Ireland. Here, Monty sees the ingenuity, passion and determination of British people to garden and create gardens, no matter what the canvas.

programme.title: Monty Don's British Gardens
programme.desc: Monty travels across the middle of the country, from Wales to Norfolk. He comes across gardens for gnomes, much-loved, well-tended allotments and extravagant gardens that bankrupted their owners.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: September is the perfect time for planting and Monty adds some autumn flowering asters to his borders as well as refreshing his strawberry beds with new stock. Carol Klein continues her series on plant families and this week she takes a close look at one of the largest plant families in the world, the daisy family, and we pay a visit to a plant nursery in Hampshire, which specialises in growing plants - including many varieties of daisy - for attracting wildlife.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: There's plenty of advice from Longmeadow this week, as Monty starts to tidy up the garden for autumn and reviews this year's display in the jewel garden. In the final part of Carol Klein's series on plant families she looks at the Apiaceae family, which includes not only stalwart and wild-life friendly plants but also edible roots; and we pay a visit to a giant vegetable grower in Nottinghamshire who is hoping to break the world record with his carrots.

programme.title: Deer and Deer Hunting
programme.desc: In this in-depth episode of Deer & Deer Hunting TV, the team explores what it truly takes to implement an advanced food plot strategy. We go beyond the basics, walking viewers through soil science, crop rotation, plot placement, and how to integrate food plots into a larger habitat management plan.

programme.title: Deer and Deer Hunting
programme.desc: Whether you're hunting 10 acres or 1,000, how you handle boundaries, buck movement, and communication with neighboring hunters can make or break your season. The team shares real-world scenarios and proven strategies to foster cooperation, avoid conflict, and build relationships that lead to better deer hunting for everyone.

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: CA heads to Jacksonville, FL to fish the deep waters of the St. Johns River with his good friend Eddie Cabbler.

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: CA Richardson travels to Wrightsville Beach, NC to fish for flounder with renowned guide Jot Owens, breaking down key techniques along the way.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In part 1 of the British episodes, Andy starts his road trip around the United Kingdom in the Cotswolds where he sets out to hunt and taste the beautiful venison that Michelin star chef, Mark Robinson has to offer. He then proceeds to travel to the border of Scotland where he samples some of the finest and most historic bird shooting practices found throughout the British Isles.

programme.title: Duck Camp Dinners
programme.desc: In this episode the DCD crew heads to the lesser-known but highly productive Red River in Western Louisiana. Despite a slow start to the season, the Red River provides some of the best hunting yet. With mixed bags of woodies, greenheads, and teal. Not to mention a handful of bonus squirrels and wild mushrooms. Shoot straight and come hungry.

programme.title: Duck Camp Dinners
programme.desc: Duck hunting in Louisiana is as much about its history as it is about its future. In the season's final days, Jean-Paul learns about Louisiana’s rich waterfowling history, dives into conservation projects throughout the state, and returns to Bayou Black for one last ride. Shoot straight and come hungry because it's the last hunt of the season.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Randy is getting the last food plot of the season in down at the Dirty Bird in Oklahoma. Then opening day of the 2025 bow season starts off in an unusual way for the crew. Randy smokes a Hog and Nate Has a really close call with one of the target bucks.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: It’s still opening week of bow season in Oklahoma and the crew has three tags left to fill.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Nate catches up with the Oklahoma buck that he had the close call with, and Cody is able to close the chapter with a buck he has been after for a few seasons.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: The boys are still at the Dirty bird in Oklahoma, trying to get a few more tasks done before heading back to the Raven in Missouri. They also manage to get out and do a little velvet scouting with the Hawk spotting scope.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Join in as Randy and Cody are hard at work on the Raven Planting some Big Tine borderline on one of their favorite plots on the farm. Then learn all about their summer time clover plot maintenance routine.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Randy, Cody, and Big Rig are still in Oklahoma with a long list of work to get done before fall comes. From moving blinds around to filling feeders the work never ends.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: The boys are at the Dirty Bird in Oklahoma trying to run some trot lines on the North Canadian river but the water is just a little lower than they expected.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: With two weeks until the opener of Missouri archery season, Randy and Cody are trying to do everything they can to have the Raven prepped for the first bow camp.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton and Rex link up with Gehrig Dieter and Anthony Sherman from the Kansas City Chiefs. While filling the boat with crappie they got to learn about the guys and their careers in the NFL.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: The Southards are heads to Table Rock for the annual family vacation. Receding water poses a challenge but there’s no lack of fish or laughs at the lake!

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton stays close to home and heads to the Lake of The Ozarks joining Austin Culbertson for a frigid couple days of fishing. The cold weather doesn’t hold back the fish, though, as they target brush piles with jerk baits and umbrella rigs.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton and Trevor pack up and drive down to Arkansas for their first experience on the White River. The brothers spend a day in the boat with Bryant Stark of Rising Rivers, fishing the White and the Norfork. Though the White is much larger waters than back home, Trevor utilizes his experience to catch them even while wading.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: About 2-3 months ago Mark purchased a 300+ acre farm in Kentucky. After running trail cameras and putting food plots on the farm, Mark now has the chance to get in the stand on opening day of Kentucky's bow season. There have been plenty of shooter bucks on the farm but only a few still are holding their velvet. Mark has his sights set on a Giant Kentucky Velvet Whitetail. The day before opening day Salt River puts on a celebration that features the @theswonbrothers.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: It's 2016 and Mark heads down to Kansas for an early season Muzzleloader Whitetail hunt with Salt River Outfitters. It was hot in the stand with little deer movement until the moon phase switched and one of the bucks Mark had been looking for stepped out. Kansas is one of those states that has an early Muzzleloader season that opens in September, and if you can get the weather and the moon to align you can catch a big deer on his feet in daylight.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: If you ask Mark Peterson what his all time most memorable hunt is, he would likely say that it's anything he's done with his kids. That answer would quickly be followed up with "Blue Sheep Hunting in Pakistan, hands down". Here, Mark takes you back to 2017 to the first (and still only) filmed hunt for Blue Sheep in Northern Pakistan. It's ironic that his most challenging and physically brutal hunt is also the one that holds the number one spot in Mark's hunting career. Enjoy this second of three episodes as he pays tribute to Sheep Week, 2022. Click the link below to join and learn more about The Wild Sheep Foundation.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Join Mark Peterson on an epic hunting adventure in the stunning landscapes of New Zealand as he goes after Whitetail deer. On this hunt, Mark was unable to get his firearms import permit in time to take his Gunwerks rifle, so he'd have to borrow a trusty rifle from his guide, Shawn. Get ready for heart-pounding action, breathtaking scenery, and valuable tips and tricks from a seasoned hunter.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark continues his quest in New Zealand, but this time, after a Wapiti. On this hunt, Mark was unable to get his firearms import permit in time to take his Gunwerks rifle, so he'd again have to borrow from his guide, Shawn. Elk were first introduced to New Zealand in 1909 when President Roosevelt gifted a herd of 20 animals that were released on the South Island. Get ready to immerse yourself in the beauty of nature that New Zealand has to offer.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: It took a couple days longer than expected, but Mark Peterson finally gets species number 14, the Knob-Billed duck while hunting in Peru. After a couple of close calls, this one was the definition of adapt and overcome. Mark and the crew had to first find the birds, then learn what it was going to take to get into the right position in order to capitalize. Of course, there was some fast mid-day action along the way to keep spirits high.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: Turkey hunting in Texas hits differently — especially when it’s your first time behind the gun. In this week’s HuntStand TURKEY POSSE episode, Will Cooper introduces longtime best friends Aubry Gold and Chance Eckert to the world of spring turkey hunting in the Lone Star State. What starts as a casual hunt quickly turns into full-blown addiction as Rio Grande gobblers fire up, decoys get worked, and the Texas cedars come alive.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: After an epic hunt with POSSE ring leader Josh Dahlke in Minnesota, where Will Cooper bagged his first Eastern gobbler and turkey outside of Texas, he heads to Pennsylvania for round two! With HuntStand in hand and the crew from Bearded Buck, they head out on morning one to find gobbles thundering off in all directions.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: Few moments in turkey hunting are better than watching a longbeard pitch down at first light. In this episode of Turkey POSSE, the crew sets up on roosted birds in Wisconsin and the action unfolds fast. At flydown, two fired-up gobblers commit to the setup, giving us an unforgettable sunrise in the spring turkey woods. Roosted birds don’t always cooperate — but when they do, it’s pure magic. If you love spring turkey hunting, hard gobbling longbeards, and classic flydown action, this is the kind of hunt that keeps us coming back every season.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: The TURKEY POSSE is on a three-state turkey hunting quest in the Northeast. Josh Dahlke stops in Maine and goes after a sneaky river-bottom gobbler.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: It’s the tail end of spring turkey season in Pennsylvania and the Turkey POSSE joins forces with friends from the Bearded Buck. Despite having access to prime turkey ground, it’s a tough hunt with tight-beaked birds that are fading toward their summer pattern. In the end, patience prevails as a group of gobblers quietly strolls into range of a well-tuned Savage Renegauge.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: Josh Dahlke makes the long-awaited journey to Hawaii as a critical piece to the Super Slam puzzle. Hawaii offers a turkey hunting adventure like none other, and there’s nobody resident companion than Jon Sabati – a Hawaii turkey hunting legend.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: In this episode of Turkey POSSE, Will Cooper follows Hudson Lochte through the Texas Hill Country as the young gun chases hard gobbling longbeards and knocks down multiple birds during an action-packed Spring turkey hunt. If you love turkey hunting, youth hunters, and classic Texas Hill Country action, this one delivers.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: Will Cooper flies from sunny Texas to chilly Minnesota for his first out-of-state turkey hunt. After an ice-cold morning in the turkey woods, the temps begin to rise and the POSSE regroups to find a midday gobbler that’s willing to work.

programme.title: Dirt
programme.desc: Guadalajara’s heritage runs deep. On the DIRT crew’s latest adventure, they explored the region’s rich history from volcanic highlands to secret surf coves. Chefs Xrysw Ruelas and Óscar Segundo sent Josh and co. to load up on locally grown coffee, spearfish between catching waves, and follow freshly harvested agave on its journey to becoming tequila. After learning the ropes of Mexico’s national sport, Charreria, and hitting up a Lucha Libre match, all the local flavors came together in a feast backed by thousands of years of tradition. There really is something special in the soil here.

programme.title: Huckberry Homes
programme.desc: On this episode of Huckberry Homes, we caught our good friend and photographer James Barkman out in the wild. He gave us a tour of his vintage "Yellow Submarine" inspired VW Van and broke down some of the modifications he has made to make this adventure bus feel more like home.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger take off from the beautiful Ocean's Edge Marina in Key West, Florida.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger meet up with Brice Barr to head out into the beautiful waters of Gulf in search of giants.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: The waters surrounding Ocean's Edge Resort on Key West, Florida provide no shortage of beautiful fishing area. Capt Scot Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger meet up with good friend and commercial fisherman John Buckheim of Keys Fresh Seafood market to haul in some delicious sport fish.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Scott and Steve take of from the georgeous Sailfish Marina in Palm Beach, FL to hunt down big ones in the waters off the Florida Coast

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captain Scott Walker and Captain Steve Rodger meet up with Dave Fields for a beautiful calm morning at Sailfish Marina. The trio run into a multitude of sport fish species and see just all of what the Florida Keys have to offer.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captain Scott Walker fishes out of Hawks Cay with fellow Duck Key resident Captain Ariel Medero catching Blackfin Tunas and big Queen Snappers.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: The sharks won’t stop Capt. Jared and special Guest Capt. Jimmy Hendricks from having their day.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: Capt. Jared Raskob joins legendary Captains Donny Lange and Charlie Scoble offshore in Islamorada.

programme.title: The Direction
programme.desc: Mark is with guest and great friend Jimmy Jennings. Jimmy helped get The Direction, Marks dream of filming outdoor TV, to reality. His team at One Call Concepts supported Marks idea of delivering The Call 811, the national call before you dig number and safe digging message, to the outdoor viewer thru outdoor TV a reality. It was Mark’s dream as a kit to host an outdoor show. Jimmy and his team took a risk that the show would take off and it did. Now airing on multiple outdoor networks and digital distribution platforms, Jimmy’s belief and Mark’s crazy dream has taken the safe digging message to tens of millions of viewers over the last 9 years. Listen to these two reminisce on past fishing trips and watch as they go to one of Mark’s favorite fishing lakes in KY for some crazy fall largemouth bass fishing action!

programme.title: LBP Spotlight
programme.desc: Kylee Short, an Orlando based bass angler, shares how she discovered fishing later in life. This LBP Spotlight highlights her journey on the water and strong family support.

programme.title: Operation Healing Heroes
programme.desc: U.S. Coast Guard helicopter pilot Shay Williams recounts his career flying countless search and rescue missions.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: With a free day before Icast. Trenton and Ryan go offshore to try and fill the cooler. Catching a variety of species and hoping to get their hands on a hogfish.

programme.title: Saltwater Underground
programme.desc: Huge Sheepshead lurk in Jersey's waters. Nick heads out with Capt. Dan Schafer to ply trophy sheeps from the bridge pilings.

programme.title: Local Lines
programme.desc: Brandon fishes with Robbie Crosnoe On Lake Henderson. These longtime buddies are dialed into the lake like no one else.

programme.title: Beyond The Surface
programme.desc: In this episode, Capt. Danny leaves the boat at home, in pursuit of some land-based fishing! Joined by his camera man for a day-off on the beautiful beaches of Anna Maria Island; the two are looking to tug on some ghostly beach snook, a true summertime classic!

programme.title: Baitfuel Fishing Tournament
programme.desc: The final day of the Baitfuel Fishing Tour Championship starts at Joel Stone Heritage Park in Gananoque, Ontario where a winner will be crowned and one luck team will be walking away with a Bass Cat / Mercury Package.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: Capt. Jared travels to Venice, Louisiana, where the Mississippi River meets the Gulf, joining Capt. Garrett George and Capt. Jesse Leboeuf for an action-packed offshore adventure in pursuit of Redfish and Tuna.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Over a decade after the game changing Longbeard XR was introduced by Winchester, the wait is finally over for their next ground breaking turkey load. After years of perfecting the recipe behind the scenes, Winchester finally unveiled the new Longbeard TSS at a turkey camp in northern Michigan. First watch the jaw dropping Longbeard TSS pattern board testing, then see it go to work on some spring gobblers.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Nestled in the hills of Ohio just west of the Appalachians is a very special deer camp ripe with memories from deer season’s past. With the development of the 350 Legend straight-walled cartridge by Winchester in 2019, a world of opportunities now existed in states like Ohio and Michigan. In this episode, with the 350 Legend in hand, three mature whitetails meet their maker, from the same blind, over the course of three seasons.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: It’s been 60 years since AA Tracker was introduced to the shooting sports world. Since that day, an immeasurable number of AA hulls have hit the ground and countless shooters have stood center stage raising a trophy where millions of dollars have been won. Professional and recreational shooters alike have trusted AA for six decades. In this episode, load up for stories of high school, collegiate competitors and the world’s best as they bust clays with the legendary shot shell.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: For the first time in history, the World English Sporting Clays Championship was held in the United States at the incredible M&M Hunting Preserve owned by the Matarese family in New Jersey. Watch as Winchester’s best shooters vie for Gold as they compete against over 2,500 shooters and 17 countries. We’ll also join the Kids and Clays foundation in Florida as they host an event that will most certainly touch your soul.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Michael Pitts and Anthony Virga join Realtree Spring Thunder producer Matt Garger to turkey hunt on his home turf in New York's Adirondack Mountains.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Tyler Jordan heads back to his college stomping grounds at Ole Miss to hunt with Athletic Director Keith Carter and William Jones. After a tough few days they were finally able to find some birds that were willing to work!

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: 2 down, 1 to go. Anthony Virga’s the next man up for a gobbler with Michael Pitts and Matt Garger in New York’s Adirondack Mountains.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: David Blanton heads to Mississippi to hunt his friend Richard Reid's property. Setting up in the dark proves to make the difference as the birds fly down into the field!

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: David Blanton heads North to hunt turkeys with new friend Evan Putnam. David gets the true Maine experience catching lobster one evening and following up with an amazing turkey hunt.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Spring Thunder's Phillip Culpepper turkey hunts Wisconsin for late-season gobblers with United Country's Travis Hamele. They have an incredible hunt, and turkeys fall from the skies.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: An epic Gould’s turkey hunt in Mexico features high-country gobbles, close-range encounters, expert calling, and unforgettable moments on this incredible adventure.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: This week we are rolling along side the crew from Double T Outfitters. Management plays a huge role in every outfitters success every year. Bret and his team have a strong management plan in place and these guys had a blast with a group of out of state hunters.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Join us for an unforgettable Women Go Hunting experience at Legends Ranch in Michigan!

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Another great hunt this week with Intrepid Care. We have the chance to hang out with some Veterans from all over the country. We gather to tell stories, build relationships, and just enjoy the great outdoors. This trip is action packed and the guys are chasing South Texas hogs from the ground.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Jennifer Brooks has spent her life answering the call to serve. From flying Blackhawk helicopters on multiple combat missions during two tours in Iraq to now serving as an Air Medical Pilot and first responder, her story is one of courage, sacrifice, and humility. In this episode of Storytellers, we join Jennifer in the field for a hunt that offers a rare moment to slow down, reflect, and honor a life dedicated to protecting others.

programme.title: TRP

programme.title: Dirt
programme.desc: Everyone knows Colorado for its epic mountains and fresh powder. But on this episode of DIRT, we’re digging into the unexpected side of the state (UFO rumors and all). Whether surfing infinite river waves, cultivating perfect carrot seeds, or fly fishing in downtown Denver’s shadow—geeky passions run high. Follow along as we gather ingredients for a farm to table final feast equal parts foraged, free-range, and free-thinking.

programme.title: Huckberry Homes
programme.desc: If you’ve ever heard, seen, or dreamed of the term van life, there is one man to thank for that, and his name is Foster Huntington. The filmmaker, photographer, and author has a storied career that includes the legendary book, "Home is Where You Park it". We met up with Foster (out on the road naturally) and he gave us a tour of his personal off-road camping rig he takes around the country.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: David Blanton heads to Mississippi to hunt his friend Richard Reid's property. Setting up in the dark proves to make the difference as the birds fly down into the field!

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: About 2-3 months ago Mark purchased a 300+ acre farm in Kentucky. After running trail cameras and putting food plots on the farm, Mark now has the chance to get in the stand on opening day of Kentucky's bow season. There have been plenty of shooter bucks on the farm but only a few still are holding their velvet. Mark has his sights set on a Giant Kentucky Velvet Whitetail. The day before opening day Salt River puts on a celebration that features the @theswonbrothers.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: When Justin and Meredith stay positive after a series of bad breaks, it pays off in fun. If fun means fish. After all, we are in Panama City Beach.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: Florida WaterSports Jeff Lange & Capt Justin head out of PCB in the wee morning hours hoping to land a limit of tasty table fare before the window of opportunity closes. Pro tip: Electric reels save shoulders.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Every spring brings a new opportunity to take a step forward & recall the memories of seasons past. World War II Veteran and retired Judge Arden Hill has spent most of his life chasing turkeys in the hills of Tennessee. Now in his 90’s, Arden doesn’t let age stand in the way of his love for spring turkey hunting. Also: patterning turkey shotguns and hunting spring gobblers.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Winchester introduces the Shoot United brand aimed at offering useful information and tips to the millions of new shooters entering the shooting sports. New York native and Midland University competitive shotgun shooter Amy Cawley and her journey as a student-athlete is followed. Also: Theo Ribbs gives shooting tips for competition and Foster Bartholow reviews Winchester AA Diamond Grade.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Shotgun sports are experiencing unprecedented growth, with competitions across the country gaining popularity as enthusiasts vie to be the best shooters in the world. Every summer, the Amateur Trapshooting Association hosts The Grand American in Sparta, Illinois. This year marks the 125th Anniversary for the largest trap shooting competition in the world. We’ll talk with the people who make this tournament run behind the scenes and explore what makes this tournament such a special event. We’ll also look at the rich history of The Grand. On-site we’ll chat with the curator of the Trapshooting Hall of Fame and look at some of the incredible historical pieces on display in the museum. Additionally, we’ll learn about White Flyer and how the clay manufacturer has made important innovations for the shotgun sports.

programme.title: Full Strut
programme.desc: Shawn's Missouri season kicked off fast with his buddy Cole tagging a giant bird but then things started to slow down a little until he found a gobbler that was ready to fight!

programme.title: Full Strut
programme.desc: Each and every spring Ty makes it a point to spend some time hunting with his father and his daughter, but it's a rarity that he's able to hunt with them both at the same time. This year though, they were able to get out twice and the second time out was a hunt they won't soon forget!

programme.title: Deer and Deer Hunting
programme.desc: In this in-depth episode of Deer & Deer Hunting TV, the team explores what it truly takes to implement an advanced food plot strategy. We go beyond the basics, walking viewers through soil science, crop rotation, plot placement, and how to integrate food plots into a larger habitat management plan.

programme.title: Deer and Deer Hunting
programme.desc: Whether you're hunting 10 acres or 1,000, how you handle boundaries, buck movement, and communication with neighboring hunters can make or break your season. The team shares real-world scenarios and proven strategies to foster cooperation, avoid conflict, and build relationships that lead to better deer hunting for everyone.

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: CA heads to Jacksonville, FL to fish the deep waters of the St. Johns River with his good friend Eddie Cabbler.

programme.title: Flats Class

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh

Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh

programme.title: Duck Camp Dinners
programme.desc: In this episode the DCD crew heads to the lesser-known but highly productive Red River in Western Louisiana. Despite a slow start to the season, the Red River provides some of the best hunting yet. With mixed bags of woodies, greenheads, and teal. Not to mention a handful of bonus squirrels and wild mushrooms. Shoot straight and come hungry.

programme.title: Duck Camp Dinners
programme.desc: Duck hunting in Louisiana is as much about its history as it is about its future. In the season's final days, Jean-Paul learns about Louisiana’s rich waterfowling history, dives into conservation projects throughout the state, and returns to Bayou Black for one last ride. Shoot straight and come hungry because it's the last hunt of the season.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Randy is getting the last food plot of the season in down at the Dirty Bird in Oklahoma. Then opening day of the 2025 bow season starts off in an unusual way for the crew. Randy smokes a Hog and Nate Has a really close call with one of the target bucks.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: It’s still opening week of bow season in Oklahoma and the crew has three tags left to fill.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Nate catches up with the Oklahoma buck that he had the close call with, and Cody is able to close the chapter with a buck he has been after for a few seasons.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: The boys are still at the Dirty bird in Oklahoma, trying to get a few more tasks done before heading back to the Raven in Missouri. They also manage to get out and do a little velvet scouting with the Hawk spotting scope.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Join in as Randy and Cody are hard at work on the Raven Planting some Big Tine borderline on one of their favorite plots on the farm. Then learn all about their summer time clover plot maintenance routine.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Randy, Cody, and Big Rig are still in Oklahoma with a long list of work to get done before fall comes. From moving blinds around to filling feeders the work never ends.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: The boys are at the Dirty Bird in Oklahoma trying to run some trot lines on the North Canadian river but the water is just a little lower than they expected.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: With two weeks until the opener of Missouri archery season, Randy and Cody are trying to do everything they can to have the Raven prepped for the first bow camp.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton and Rex link up with Gehrig Dieter and Anthony Sherman from the Kansas City Chiefs. While filling the boat with crappie they got to learn about the guys and their careers in the NFL.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: The Southards are heads to Table Rock for the annual family vacation. Receding water poses a challenge but there’s no lack of fish or laughs at the lake!

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton stays close to home and heads to the Lake of The Ozarks joining Austin Culbertson for a frigid couple days of fishing. The cold weather doesn’t hold back the fish, though, as they target brush piles with jerk baits and umbrella rigs.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton and Trevor pack up and drive down to Arkansas for their first experience on the White River. The brothers spend a day in the boat with Bryant Stark of Rising Rivers, fishing the White and the Norfork. Though the White is much larger waters than back home, Trevor utilizes his experience to catch them even while wading.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: About 2-3 months ago Mark purchased a 300+ acre farm in Kentucky. After running trail cameras and putting food plots on the farm, Mark now has the chance to get in the stand on opening day of Kentucky's bow season. There have been plenty of shooter bucks on the farm but only a few still are holding their velvet. Mark has his sights set on a Giant Kentucky Velvet Whitetail. The day before opening day Salt River puts on a celebration that features the @theswonbrothers.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: It's 2016 and Mark heads down to Kansas for an early season Muzzleloader Whitetail hunt with Salt River Outfitters. It was hot in the stand with little deer movement until the moon phase switched and one of the bucks Mark had been looking for stepped out. Kansas is one of those states that has an early Muzzleloader season that opens in September, and if you can get the weather and the moon to align you can catch a big deer on his feet in daylight.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: If you ask Mark Peterson what his all time most memorable hunt is, he would likely say that it's anything he's done with his kids. That answer would quickly be followed up with "Blue Sheep Hunting in Pakistan, hands down". Here, Mark takes you back to 2017 to the first (and still only) filmed hunt for Blue Sheep in Northern Pakistan. It's ironic that his most challenging and physically brutal hunt is also the one that holds the number one spot in Mark's hunting career. Enjoy this second of three episodes as he pays tribute to Sheep Week, 2022. Click the link below to join and learn more about The Wild Sheep Foundation.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Join Mark Peterson on an epic hunting adventure in the stunning landscapes of New Zealand as he goes after Whitetail deer. On this hunt, Mark was unable to get his firearms import permit in time to take his Gunwerks rifle, so he'd have to borrow a trusty rifle from his guide, Shawn. Get ready for heart-pounding action, breathtaking scenery, and valuable tips and tricks from a seasoned hunter.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark continues his quest in New Zealand, but this time, after a Wapiti. On this hunt, Mark was unable to get his firearms import permit in time to take his Gunwerks rifle, so he'd again have to borrow from his guide, Shawn. Elk were first introduced to New Zealand in 1909 when President Roosevelt gifted a herd of 20 animals that were released on the South Island. Get ready to immerse yourself in the beauty of nature that New Zealand has to offer.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: It took a couple days longer than expected, but Mark Peterson finally gets species number 14, the Knob-Billed duck while hunting in Peru. After a couple of close calls, this one was the definition of adapt and overcome. Mark and the crew had to first find the birds, then learn what it was going to take to get into the right position in order to capitalize. Of course, there was some fast mid-day action along the way to keep spirits high.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: Turkey hunting in Texas hits differently — especially when it’s your first time behind the gun. In this week’s HuntStand TURKEY POSSE episode, Will Cooper introduces longtime best friends Aubry Gold and Chance Eckert to the world of spring turkey hunting in the Lone Star State. What starts as a casual hunt quickly turns into full-blown addiction as Rio Grande gobblers fire up, decoys get worked, and the Texas cedars come alive.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: After an epic hunt with POSSE ring leader Josh Dahlke in Minnesota, where Will Cooper bagged his first Eastern gobbler and turkey outside of Texas, he heads to Pennsylvania for round two! With HuntStand in hand and the crew from Bearded Buck, they head out on morning one to find gobbles thundering off in all directions.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: Few moments in turkey hunting are better than watching a longbeard pitch down at first light. In this episode of Turkey POSSE, the crew sets up on roosted birds in Wisconsin and the action unfolds fast. At flydown, two fired-up gobblers commit to the setup, giving us an unforgettable sunrise in the spring turkey woods. Roosted birds don’t always cooperate — but when they do, it’s pure magic. If you love spring turkey hunting, hard gobbling longbeards, and classic flydown action, this is the kind of hunt that keeps us coming back every season.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: The TURKEY POSSE is on a three-state turkey hunting quest in the Northeast. Josh Dahlke stops in Maine and goes after a sneaky river-bottom gobbler.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: It’s the tail end of spring turkey season in Pennsylvania and the Turkey POSSE joins forces with friends from the Bearded Buck. Despite having access to prime turkey ground, it’s a tough hunt with tight-beaked birds that are fading toward their summer pattern. In the end, patience prevails as a group of gobblers quietly strolls into range of a well-tuned Savage Renegauge.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: Josh Dahlke makes the long-awaited journey to Hawaii as a critical piece to the Super Slam puzzle. Hawaii offers a turkey hunting adventure like none other, and there’s nobody resident companion than Jon Sabati – a Hawaii turkey hunting legend.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: In this episode of Turkey POSSE, Will Cooper follows Hudson Lochte through the Texas Hill Country as the young gun chases hard gobbling longbeards and knocks down multiple birds during an action-packed Spring turkey hunt. If you love turkey hunting, youth hunters, and classic Texas Hill Country action, this one delivers.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: Will Cooper flies from sunny Texas to chilly Minnesota for his first out-of-state turkey hunt. After an ice-cold morning in the turkey woods, the temps begin to rise and the POSSE regroups to find a midday gobbler that’s willing to work.

programme.title: Dirt
programme.desc: Guadalajara’s heritage runs deep. On the DIRT crew’s latest adventure, they explored the region’s rich history from volcanic highlands to secret surf coves. Chefs Xrysw Ruelas and Óscar Segundo sent Josh and co. to load up on locally grown coffee, spearfish between catching waves, and follow freshly harvested agave on its journey to becoming tequila. After learning the ropes of Mexico’s national sport, Charreria, and hitting up a Lucha Libre match, all the local flavors came together in a feast backed by thousands of years of tradition. There really is something special in the soil here.

programme.title: Huckberry Homes
programme.desc: On this episode of Huckberry Homes, we caught our good friend and photographer James Barkman out in the wild. He gave us a tour of his vintage "Yellow Submarine" inspired VW Van and broke down some of the modifications he has made to make this adventure bus feel more like home.

programme.title: The Direction
programme.desc: Mark is with guest and great friend Jimmy Jennings. Jimmy helped get The Direction, Marks dream of filming outdoor TV, to reality. His team at One Call Concepts supported Marks idea of delivering The Call 811, the national call before you dig number and safe digging message, to the outdoor viewer thru outdoor TV a reality. It was Mark’s dream as a kit to host an outdoor show. Jimmy and his team took a risk that the show would take off and it did. Now airing on multiple outdoor networks and digital distribution platforms, Jimmy’s belief and Mark’s crazy dream has taken the safe digging message to tens of millions of viewers over the last 9 years. Listen to these two reminisce on past fishing trips and watch as they go to one of Mark’s favorite fishing lakes in KY for some crazy fall largemouth bass fishing action!

programme.desc: Kylee Short, an Orlando based bass angler, shares how she discovered fishing later in life. This LBP Spotlight highlights her journey on the water and strong family support.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Over a decade after the game changing Longbeard XR was introduced by Winchester, the wait is finally over for their next ground breaking turkey load. After years of perfecting the recipe behind the scenes, Winchester finally unveiled the new Longbeard TSS at a turkey camp in northern Michigan. First watch the jaw dropping Longbeard TSS pattern board testing, then see it go to work on some spring gobblers.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Nestled in the hills of Ohio just west of the Appalachians is a very special deer camp ripe with memories from deer season’s past. With the development of the 350 Legend straight-walled cartridge by Winchester in 2019, a world of opportunities now existed in states like Ohio and Michigan. In this episode, with the 350 Legend in hand, three mature whitetails meet their maker, from the same blind, over the course of three seasons.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: It’s been 60 years since AA Tracker was introduced to the shooting sports world. Since that day, an immeasurable number of AA hulls have hit the ground and countless shooters have stood center stage raising a trophy where millions of dollars have been won. Professional and recreational shooters alike have trusted AA for six decades. In this episode, load up for stories of high school, collegiate competitors and the world’s best as they bust clays with the legendary shot shell.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: For the first time in history, the World English Sporting Clays Championship was held in the United States at the incredible M&M Hunting Preserve owned by the Matarese family in New Jersey. Watch as Winchester’s best shooters vie for Gold as they compete against over 2,500 shooters and 17 countries. We’ll also join the Kids and Clays foundation in Florida as they host an event that will most certainly touch your soul.

programme.title: Spring Thunder

programme.title: Spring Thunder
